rulings.Case Title: ZHONGYING DING VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion To Quash Trial Subpoena Of Jeffrey Bramlett (Per Ex Parte Order 5/3/19)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, line 14. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion To Quash Trial Subpoena Of Jeffrey Bramlett. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to quash trial subpoena of Jeffrey Bramlett is denied. The City's counsel previously agreed to accept service of the subpoena on behalf of Mr. Bramlett, who is a material witness who signed the City's written discovery responses and a declaration in support of the City's motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff Zhongying Ding is not seeking to reopen discovery or to depose Mr. Bramlett. The City can readily resolve Mr. Bramlett's schedule conflict with the current trial date by accepting Plaintiff's offer to stipulate to a brief continuance of the trial until Mr. Bramlett returns from vacation. rulings.Case Title: TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES LLP VS. LAMA ASHKAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, line 4. PLAINTIFF TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES LLP'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal. Plaintiff Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP's motion to set aside the order dismissing this case filed on November 20, 2018 is granted. The court notes that Defendant Lama Ashkar states that she was not served with the motion, and the address that appears on Plaintiff's proof of service appears to be incorrect (misspelled street name, wrong zip code). Plaintiff has shown that the order dismissing the case was entered as a result of counsel's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect due to a calendaring error and counsel's absence on maternity leave. (Code Civ. Proc. § 473(b).) Although Plaintiff has not fully explained why it delayed as long as it did in bringing the motion, Defendant has not shown that she will be prejudiced by reinstating the action for the sole purpose of enabling Plaintiff to seek summary enforcement of the parties' settlement agreement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, assuming such relief is available to it and is warranted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BIANA CHERNOGUZ ET AL VS. LEANA PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Limit Scope Of The Depositions Of Defendants/Cross-Complainants Leana Perez And Francisco Armas
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTS LEANA PEREZ AND FRANCISCO ARMAS' Motion For Protective Order To Limit Scope Of The Depositions Of Defendants/Cross-Complainants Leana Perez And Francisco Armas Judge Pro Tem Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs/cross-defendant Cernoguz may depose Defendants/Cross-complainants Leana Perez and Francisco Armas regarding facts related to the subject matter of the remaining cause of action in the complaint for nuisance, and regarding facts related the subject matter of the seven (7) causes of action alleged in the Perez/Armas cross-complaint. The Court issues a protective order precluding deposition questioning into the creation of the tenancy, or whether there was a legal tenancy by Perez and Armas, as these issues have been adjudicated before the rent board, whose decision is now on appeal. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY BRUNECKER VS. MARIN COUNTRY DAY SCHOOL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Oppose Cross Defendants Application That Settlement Was Made In Good Faith
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, line 5. DEFENDANTS MARIN COUNTRY DAY SCHOOL, STEVEN HARRISON'S Motion To Oppose Cross Defendants Application That Settlement Was Made In Good Faith. Cross-defendants Tsenjjiav Jigjidsuren and Sergelen Ganutumur's application for a good faith settlement determination of their settlement with plaintiff Jeffrey Brunecker pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 877.6 is granted and Defendants Marin Country Day School and Steven Harrison's motion in opposition is denied. Cross-defendants have standing to seek a good faith determination of their settlement with plaintiff, even though they did not make an appearance as defendants before they were dismissed by plaintiff. (See Great Western Bank v. Converse Consultants, Inc. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 609, 613 ["A cross-defendant who has not been named as a defendant in the main action may enter into a good faith settlement with the plaintiff in the main action and obtain a dismissal of the cross-complaint pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6."]; County of Los Angeles v. Guerrero (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1149, 1152 ["The fact the settlement was entered into well before the filing of suit does not preclude a finding of good faith."].) There is no bar to seeking a determination after the finalization of the settlement. (See Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 822, 829 ["Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6 provides no time limit after settlement within which a motion for determination of good faith of the settlement must be brought."].) Defendants have not met their burden to show that the settlement was not entered into in good faith. (Code Civ. Proc. § 877.6(d).) The court finds that the amount of the settlement based on the information available to the settling parties at time of settlement is not grossly disproportionate to a reasonable estimate of settling cross-defendants' liability and that the settlement was the result of arm's-length bargaining in good faith. (Helser Decl. 5, 6; see Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for cross-defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN REED VS. TRANSPORTATION BROKERAGE SPECIALISTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendants Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, line 6. PLAINTIFF JOHN REED'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendants Motion To Compel Arbitration. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Plaintiff John Reed's motion for reconsideration of the December 17, 2018 order granting defendant Transportation Brokerage Specialists, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is granted. The December 17, 2018 order is vacated and a new order is entered denying TBS's motion to compel arbitration. Zakaryan v. The Men's Wearhouse, Inc. (2019) 33 Cal. App. 5th 659 constitutes "new or different law" within the meaning of CCP 1008(a). Even if it did not, Mr. Reed's motion is treated as an invitation to the court to reconsider the December 17, 2018 order and the court exercises its discretion per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094 to do so. When TBS' motion to compel arbitration was heard and decided on December 17, 2018, the only on-point authority binding on this court on the issue of whether Mr. Reed's PAGA claim is arbitrable was Esparza v. KS Industries, L.P. (2017) 13 Cal. App. 5th 1228 which required this court to grant TBS' motion. Per CRC 8.1115(e)(1), because a petition for review had been granted and was pending, Lawson v. ZB, N.A. (2018) 18 Cal. App. 5th 705 had "no binding or precedential effect." With the issuance of Zakaryan, there are now two on-point authorities with full precedential effect, but neither is binding on this court because Esparza and Zakaryan reach conflicting results based on conflicting reasoning and are not reconcilable. This court "must make a choice between the conflicting decisions." (Auto Equity Sales, Inc. v. Superior Court (1962) 57 Cal. 2d 450, 456). Because Zakaryan is better-reasoned and comports better with Iskanian v. CLS Transportation Los Angeles, LLC (2014) 59 Cal. 4th 348 than Esparza, this court chooses to follow Zakaryan rather than Esparza. Zakaryan requires that TBS' motion to compel arbitration be denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Reed is required to prepare a proposed order which is entitled "Order Granting Plaintiff's Motion for Reconsideration of the December 17, 2018 Order, Vacating That Order, and Denying Defendant's Motion to Compel Arbitration," repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. =(302/HEK)

DEFENDANTS AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY, ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Joint)/Adjudication, Against Plaintiff National Union Fire Insurance Company Of Pittsburgh, Pa, And Cross-Complainant, Arup North America, Ltd. rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF VS. AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, line 7. DEFENDANT ARUP NORTH AMERICA LTD'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS. *** Tentative Ruling - Part 1 of 2 *** Defendant and cross-complainant Arup North America Ltd.'s ("Arup") motion to stay entire action is granted. When the court previously issued a stay of discovery in this case rather than a complete stay, it believed that the coverage issues could possibly be resolved based on the underlying pleadings alone. At that time, the record was unclear whether resolution of the coverage issues could lead to a risk of inconsistent factual determinations that could prejudice Arup. The court now determines that there is a risk of inconsistent factual determinations and a stay of the entire action is warranted. "To eliminate the risk of inconsistent factual determinations that could prejudice the insured, a stay of the declaratory relief action pending resolution of the third party suit is appropriate when the coverage question turns on facts to be litigated in the underlying action. . . . By contrast, when the coverage question is logically unrelated to the issues of consequence in the underlying case, the declaratory relief judgment may properly proceed to judgment." (Montrose Chemical Corp. v. Superior Court (1993) 6 Cal.4th 287, 301.) "A court considering whether to stay a declaratory relief action must therefore consider precisely which issues are to be litigated in order to resolve the declaratory relief action, and whether those issues are related to factual issues yet to be litigated in the underlying action. [Citation.] If the factual issues to be resolved in the declaratory relief action overlap with issues to be resolved in the underlying litigation, the trial court must stay the declaratory relief action." (Great American Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 221, 235 (emphasis in original).) Here, the character and scope of Arup's work is a factual issue that will be litigated in both actions. Zurich relies on extrinsic evidence, including Arup's discovery responses in the underlying action, to support its assertion that Arup provided only professional services. (Arup's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. H, Zurich's separate statement of undisputed facts 98-106.) Arup is possibly prejudiced because the plaintiffs in the underlying actions might use the evidence that Arup presents to show that its work involved more than professional services and this Court's findings against Arup. "[T]he insurer must not be permitted to join forces with the plaintiffs in the underlying actions as a means to defeat coverage." (Montrose Chemical Corp. v. Superior Court (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 902, 910.) The potential conflict mandates granting a stay of the entire action. (Montrose, 25 Cal.App.4th at 910 ["In a case where there is no potential conflict between the coverage issues and the issues in the third party action, the carrier may obtain an early trial date in the coverage action." (emphasis added)]; see also United Enterprises, Inc. v. Superior Court (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1004, 1010-1012 [trial court erred in denying motion for stay where factual issues to be resolved in the declaratory relief action overlapped factual issues to be resolved in the underlying actions].) *** Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2 *** =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF VS. AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against National Union Fire Insurance Company Of Pittsburgh Pa
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, line 7. DEFENDANT ARUP NORTH AMERICA LTD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against National Union Fire Insurance Company Of Pittsburgh Pa. Off calendar because the action is stayed. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF VS. AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Arup North America Ltd'S Motion To Stay Proceedings (Added For Purposes Of Entering Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Arup North America Ltd's Motion To Stay Proceedings *** Tentative Ruling - Part 2 of 2 *** The Court is sympathetic with National Union's desire to obtain a quick resolution of its coverage obligation because it is currently expending funds and defending Arup in the underlying actions. A partial stay is not appropriate, however. National Union can seek equitable contribution against Zurich once the underlying actions are resolved. Moreover, a partial stay that allows National Union's motion to proceed is not feasible. Zurich raises the same extrinsic evidence regarding the scope and character of Arup's work in its joint opposition to National Union's and Arup's motions for summary adjudication. (Zurich's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. C, Zurich's joint opposition, 16:9-24.) Therefore, a partial stay raises the same specter of inconsistent factual findings. Finally, the court notes that on the current schedule, Arup's motions for summary judgment in the underlying cases are set for hearing on August 9, 2019; thus, a stay of this action will not necessarily be a lengthy one. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Arup is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. *** End of Tentative Ruling *** =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DENTAL FORCE, INC. ET AL VS. BEMDAD BEHNOOD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bamdad Behnood'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, line 10. DEFENDANT BAMDAD BEHNOOD'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint. Defendant Bemdad Behnood's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. Even if the cross-complaint is not compulsory as to Plaintiff Dental Force, Inc., the motion is supported by California's policy of liberality in pleading and by the interest in judicial economy. (Code Civ. Proc. § 428.50(c); see City of Hanford v. Superior Court (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 580, 587 ["The reason for allowing cross-complaints is to have a complete determination of a controversy among the parties in one action, thus avoiding circuity of action and duplication of time and effort."].) Defendant has not delayed unduly in seeking leave to file her cross-complaint, and Plaintiffs have not shown that they would be prejudiced if the Court were to grant the motion. Plaintiffs may raise any objections they have to the allegations in the proposed cross-complaint on summary judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: NATHANIEL FARBER VS. HYRECAR INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Joinder Or, In The Alternative, To Consolidate Cases
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, line 11. DEFENDANT HYRECAR INC.'S Motion To Compel Joinder Or, In The Alternative, To Consolidate Cases. Defendant Hyrecar Inc.'s motion to compel joinder is denied and its alternative motion to consolidate case numbers CGC-18-571257, Nathaniel Farber vs. Hyrecar Inc. and CGC-18-571258, Josiah Larkin vs. Hyrecar Inc., is granted. The fact that the plaintiffs in both actions are parties to the same contract with defendant HyreCar does not require joinder of both plaintiffs, absent a showing that they are necessary parties for each other's actions under the statutory factors set forth in Code of Civil Procedure section 389(a). Here, complete relief between HyreCar and Mr. Farber and HyreCar and Mr. Larkin can be accorded without joining Mr. Farber and Mr. Larkin in a single action as co-plaintiffs. (Countrywide Home Loans, Inc. v. Superior Court (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 785, 794 ["the term complete relief refers only 'to relief as between the persons already parties, and not as between a party and the absent person whose joinder is sought.'"]). While HyreCar may be exposed to inconsistent rulings on its contractual obligations if the plaintiffs are not joined, HyreCar does not risk being unable to comply with its obligations towards plaintiffs or of suffering multiple liability. (See Van Zant v. Apple Inc. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 965, 977 ["'[I]inconsistent obligations occur when a party is unable to comply with one court's order without breaching another court's order concerning the same incident.' [Citation.] In contrast, inconsistent adjudications or results occur when a party wins on a claim in one forum and loses on another claim from the same incident in another forum.'"].) It is undisputed, however, that the two actions involve almost identical questions of law and fact and would result in unnecessary inefficiencies and create a risk of inconsistent adjudication if ligated separately. (See In re Marriage of Davis (1977) 68 Cal.App.3d 294, 304 ["The law abhors multiplicity of actions, consuming the time of the court and entailing additional expense to the people."]). There is no danger that consolidating the actions will result in confusion of the jury. Any concern regarding prejudice resulting from the introduction of evidence of Mr. Larkin's criminal conviction may be addressed in the trial court through motions in limine. Therefore, the cases are consolidated for all purposes with CGC-18-571257 as the lead case; however, separate verdicts and judgments shall be entered as to each plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for HyreCar is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants General Tso Kitchen, Inc., And Li Cao'S Motion For An Order Establishing Admissions Or Compelling Responses And Further Responses, And Compelling Responses And Further Responses To Their First Set Of Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions [Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, Line 2, [Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling] Defendants General Tso Kitchen, Inc., And Li Cao'S Motion For An Order Establishing Admissions Or Compelling Responses And Further Responses, And Compelling Responses And Further Responses To Their First Set Of Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions With regard to form interrogatories, San Dong Best Inc. and Shubo Wang shall within 14 days provide responses to all form interrogatories to which they did not respond. Sanctions denied as to both sides. In particular, with regard to RFAs, no sanctions of any kind can be imposed for mere delay in responding to RFAs if no motion has yet been filed. (St. Mary v. Superior Court (2014) 223 CA4th 762, 784.) The motion to have matters deemed admitted was thus a non-starter before it was even filed. The Court finds that both sides made mistakes in this discovery dispute, and this will not be rewarded with sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renotice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Establishing Admissions Or Compelling Responses And Further Responses, And Compelling Responses And Further Responses To Their First Set Of Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 16, 2019, Line 2, [PART 1 OF 2 FOR TENTATIVE RULING] DEFENDANTS GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC., AND LI CAO'S Motion For An Order Establishing Admissions Or Compelling Responses And Further Responses, And Compelling Responses And Further Responses To Their First Set Of Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions Judge Pro Tem Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part, denied in part. The Court finds based on the LaLanne Declaration, and not contradicted by the Sarkar declaration, that the written discovery was hand served on February 25, but was apparently left on the back deck of attorney LaLanne's office. It is debatable under CCP sec. 1011(a) whether this is proper personal service, in that it was not left with a person, and was left on the back deck. Because of how the discovery was served, it was a foreseeable and inadvertent mistake by attorney LaLanne to not realize that the discovery had been hand served and to calendar responses as if due by mail. The Court further finds that attorney LaLanne's proof of service of responses by mail is valid and that objections were not waived. The Court also finds that moving party rushed this motion and failed to engage in a substantive meet and confer effort. Moving party ignored the well-known rule that, even where responses to Request for Admissions are late, as long as responses in "substantial compliance" with CCP § 2033.220 are served prior to the hearing, the motion to establish admissions is defeated. No showing of "mistake, inadvertence or excusable neglect" is required. [Tobin v. Oris, supra, 3 CA4th at 828) The motion is therefore DENIED as to the RFAs. The Court finds that the objections to Special Interrogatories (on grounds that the interrogatories are not complete in and of themselves) are not well-stated and overrules the objections. The interrogatories are reasonably clear and the responding parties should have made an effort to provide a good faith response. The motion to compel responses is GRANTED as to all Special Interrogatories. The Court orders San Dong Best Inc. and Shubo Wang to provide complete, verified, objection-free responses within 14 days of service of this Order. The Court sustains the objections to Demand for Production No. 6 and 18, as these requests are extremely overboard and compound, and invade financial privacy. No further response is required to those requests. To the extent San Dong Best Inc. and Shubo Wang still owe documents they said they would produce in their responses to Demand For Production, the Court orders production within 14 days of service of this Order. [SEE PART 2 OF 2 FOR ENTIRE TENTATIVE RULING] =(302/JPT)
